title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: nesstarR R package reads parses NESSTAR binary containers distributed statistical agencies (e.g., MOSPI India census/survey data). decodes compact binary formats R data frames exports CSV.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Note: tests/testthat/ currently empty. Tests require actual .Nesstar binary files fixtures; none committed repository.
code: # Load package (compiles C code) Rscript -e "devtools::load_all()"  # Run all tests Rscript -e "testthat::test_dir('tests/testthat')"  # Run a single test file Rscript -e "testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test-nesstar.R')"  # Generate documentation (roxygen2) Rscript -e "roxygen2::roxygenise()"  # Full CRAN-style check R CMD check .  # Install from source R CMD INSTALL .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: nesstar_binary object: - nesstar_datasets(x): list datasets row/column counts - nesstar_variables(x, n): describe variables dataset n - nesstar_metadata(x): decode Huffman-compressed XML labels - nesstar_read_dataset(x, n, columns=NULL): read data data.frame - nesstar_export(x, output_dir, ...): export CSV.gz (chunked, default 50k rows)
code: .Nesstar binary file   → nesstar_parse()          [R: R/nesstar.R]   → .Call("nesstar_parse_binary")   → C parser                 [C: src/nesstar.c]   → nesstar_binary S3 object

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/nesstar.R: High-level R API main entry point. nesstar_parse() function reads binary file returns S3 object. Chunked export logic lives . R/metadata.R: Huffman-compressed XML metadata extraction. Byte-order-aware integer reading (.u32le, .u32be). Metadata decoding returns NULL warning older formats without XML blocks. src/nesstar.c: Three distinct responsibilities: 1. Container parsing (nesstar_parse_binary): Detects byte order offset 0x25, parses resource index (ID→offset map), extracts dataset descriptors variable directories. 2. Huffman decompression (nesstar_decode_huffman): Min-heap tree construction, bit--bit traversal decode XML metadata blocks. 3. Column decoding (nesstar_decode_column, nesstar_decode_column_range): Mode 1 = fixed-width UTF-16LE strings; Mode 5 = numeric (nibble/byte/uint16/24/32/40/float64). Row-range support enables chunked reads.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Magic bytes: "NESSTART" (8 bytes) Default byte order: little-endian; falls back big-endian Strings: UTF-16LE file, converted UTF-8 R Numeric formats: 4-bit nibble IEEE 754 float64 Resource index: maps record IDs file offsets/lengths

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Full binary format documentation (header layout, resource index record layout, variable directory fields, Huffman block structure, observed file sizes) NESSTAR_FORMAT.md.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: 5-byte (u40 LE) offsets files > 4 GB: resource index location (stored header offset 0x25) per-record target_offset fields resource index 40-bit little-endian, 32-bit. Byte 0x29 header high byte index offset; byte +8 15-byte resource record high byte target_offset. Files ≤ 4 GB bytes 0x00, 32-bit reads work accidentally. Fixed src/nesstar.c (parse_resource_index, detect_byte_order, ResRecord.target_offset). mode_code 0 variables: older NSS files contain variable directory entries mode_code = 0 (neither string/1 numeric/5). treated -NA numeric columns. Guard added R/nesstar.R (.decode_columns, .decode_columns_range) C call. R-side metadata index reads 32-bit offsets: R/metadata.R::.build_resource_index uses .u32le / .u32be, reads 4-byte offsets. C parser correctly reads 5-byte (u40) offsets. means nesstar_metadata() break files > 4 GB (e.g. 68th-round Type 1 file). fix add 5th byte raw[idx_off + 5] high byte, mirroring C logic.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: stata/: Contains reference STATA zip archives MOSPI (DDI-IND-MOSPI-NSSO-HCES22-23_STATA.zip, Nss68_STATA_2012.zip). part R package; serve reference datasets validating parser output.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: external R packages; base R tools (built-). C code compiled first install/load via src/Makevars (-Wall -Wextra -O2).

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: documents .Nesstar binary container format reverse-engineered MOSPI India HCES survey files. proprietary format produced NESSTAR Publisher software (now discontinued).

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: integers little-endian every file seen date (MOSPI HCES rounds 57–68). big-endian fallback detected heuristically observed practice.
code: [Header - 0x57 bytes] [Data blocks - variable-length, one per variable per dataset] [Huffman-compressed XML metadata blocks - optional] [Resource index - trailing, variable length]

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: Located resource_index_offset (header), end file (“trailing index”). 15-byte record: MAX_RESOURCES observed: ~1,300 4.65 GB file (68th round, Type 1).
code: [u32 LE]  count          - number of records [count × 15-byte records]

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: Located target_offset record record_id == base_record_id. Contains dataset_count entries, descriptor_record_size bytes. entry:

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: Located target_offset record record_id == variable_directory_record_id. Contains variable_count entries, variable_directory_entry_size (160) bytes. Key fields within 160-byte entry:

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: variable’s data occupies contiguous block target_offset record record_id == variable_id.

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: Fixed-width, width_value bytes per row, UTF-16LE (plain bytes, effectively ASCII/Latin-1). Row occupies [* width, * width + width).

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: Encoded value = stored_raw + value_offset_i64.

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: Variable directory entry present resource index record variable_id (.e., data_length == 0). Decoded -NA.

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: Variable labels category codes stored Huffman-compressed XML blocks. block: tree built standard min-heap Huffman tree. Single-symbol alphabets degenerate case (bits → 0 → left child repeatedly). Older NSS-format files (rounds 57–63, delivered CSV) embed metadata XML; nesstar_metadata() returns NULL .
code: [optional: 1 byte dataset-index] [1 byte]  symbol_count  (1–256) [1 byte]  reserved [1 byte]  reserved [symbol_count × 5 bytes]  symbol table: (symbol_byte u8, freq u32le) [4 bytes]  output_length u32le  (== sum of all frequencies) [bit stream, LSB-first]

title: NESSTAR Binary Format
text: 68th T1 file one observed exceed 4 GB, triggering 40-bit offset requirement.

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
code: remotes::install_github("saketkc/mospiR") remotes::install_github("saketkc/nesstarR") library(mospiR) library(nesstarR)

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: mospiR::download_dataset() authenticates MoSPI NADA API downloads full archive one survey idno. API key read MOSPI_KEY (set ~/.Renviron). download .rar archive, around 270 MB NSS 68 T2.
code: api_key  <- Sys.getenv("MOSPI_KEY") data_dir <- file.path("data", "hces",                       "DDI-IND-MOSPI-NSSO-68Rnd-Sch2.0-July2011-June2012")  download_dataset(   "DDI-IND-MOSPI-NSSO-68Rnd-Sch2.0-July2011-June2012",   data_dir,   api_key )

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: unar (macOS/Linux) unrar (Windows) work. Either must PATH. Extraction produces folder containing: survey0/data/nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2.Nesstar (binary data) ddi.xml (variable metadata DDI format, required nesstarR) Readme.pdf / supporting documents
code: rar_file <- file.path(data_dir, "Nss68_1.0_Type2_new format.rar") system2("unar", c("-o", dirname(rar_file), shQuote(rar_file)))

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: nesstar_parse() reads entire file memory decodes binary header, resource index, dataset descriptors, variable directories. row data loaded stage.
code: nb <- nesstar_parse(nesstar_path) nb #> <nesstar_binary> #>  File      : nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2.Nesstar  #>  Datasets  : 11

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: single .Nesstar container holds multiple datasets, one per schedule block (household, person, food, non-food, …). dataset largest row count food block: one row per household × item combination (~4-5 million rows NSS 68 T2).
code: nesstar_datasets(nb) #>    dataset_number row_count variable_count #> 1              47    101651             37 #> 2              48    101651             42 #> 3              49    101651             35 #> 4              50    464730             39 #> 5              51   5277850             32 #> 6              52   1493313             29 #> 7              53    370071             28 #> 8              54   2343672             28 #> 9              55   3436786             36 #> 10             56   3317537             28 #> 11             57    101647             41 ds_tbl <- nesstar_datasets(nb) food_ds <- ds_tbl$dataset_number[which.max(ds_tbl$row_count)] cat("Food block dataset number:", food_ds, "\n") #> Food block dataset number: 51 cat("Rows:", ds_tbl$row_count[ds_tbl$dataset_number == food_ds], "\n") #> Rows: 5277850

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
code: vars <- nesstar_variables(nb, dataset_number = food_ds) vars[, c("name", "mode_code", "value_format_code", "width_value")] #>                           name mode_code value_format_code width_value #> 1            Round_Centre_Code         1                 2           3 #> 2                FSU_Serial_No         1                 2           5 #> 3                        Round 2 #> 9                      Stratum         1                 2           2 #> 10              Sub_Stratum_No         1                 2           2 #> 11               Schedule_type         1 FOD_Sub_region         1                 2           4 #> 15 Hamlet_Group_Sub_Stratum_no         1                 2           1 #> 16     Second_Stage_Stratum_No         1                 2           1 #> 17              Sample_hhld_no         1                 2           2 #> 18                       Level         1                 2           2 #> 19                   Item_Code         1                 2           3 #> 20       Home_Produce_Quantity         5                 5           7 #> 21          Home_Produce_Value         5                 4           5 #> 22  Total_Consumption_Quantity         5                 5           7 #> 23     Total_Consumption_Value         5                 5           5 #> 24                 Source_Code         1                 2           1 #> 25                         NSS         5                 3           2 #> 26                         NSC         5                 3           2 #> 27                         MLT title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: NESSTAR files NSS 64 onwards embed Huffman-compressed XML blocks variable labels category codes.
code:
meta <- nesstar_metadata(nb) #> Warning in nesstar_metadata(nb): Embedded metadata record ids 1 and 2 not #> found; this file uses an older format. Labels are unavailable. if (!is.null(meta)) {   ds_meta <- meta$datasets[[which(     vapply(meta$datasets, `[[`, integer(1), "dataset_number") == food_ds   )]]   cat("File name stored in container:", ds_meta$file_name, "

")    for (v in head(ds_meta$variables, 6)) {     cat(sprintf("%-20s  label: %s
", v$name, v$label))     if (length(v$categories) > 0) {       for (cat_entry in head(v$categories, 4)) {         cat(sprintf("  %s = %s
", cat_entry$value, cat_entry$label))       }     }   } } else {   message("Metadata not available for this file (older NSS format).") } #> Metadata not available for this file (older NSS format).

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: nesstar_read_dataset() decodes columns requested dataset. food block slow step (~10-30 s laptop): 4–5 million rows × ~20 columns, decoded packed binary format. Pass columns load need:
code: food <- nesstar_read_dataset(   nb,   dataset_number = food_ds,   columns = c("HHID", "State_code", "District_Code", "Sector",               "Combined_Multiplier", "Item_Code", "Total_Consumption_Value") ) cat("Rows:", nrow(food), "| Columns:", ncol(food), "\n") #> Rows: 5277850 | Columns: 7 head(food) #>   Sector Item_Code Total_Consumption_Value      HHID State_code District_Code #> 1      1       101                     420 715581201         17          1701 #> 2      1       102                     255 715581201         17          1701 #> 3      1       105                      15 715581201         17          1701 #> 4      1       113                      60 715581201         17          1701 #> 5      1       129                     750 715581201         17          1701 #> 6      1       139                      50 715581201         17          1701 #>   Combined_Multiplier #> 1              324.08 #> 2              324.08 #> 3              324.08 #> 4              324.08 #> 5              324.08 #> 6              324.08

what: Quick check: cereal spending by sector
title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: Weighted mean monthly household expenditure cereals (item codes 101–140), rural vs. urban. Multiplier column survey weight.
code:
cereals    <- food[as.integer(food$Item_Code) >= 101 &                    as.integer(food$Item_Code) <= 140, ] hh_cereals <- aggregate(Total_Consumption_Value ~ HHID + Sector + Combined_Multiplier,                         data = cereals, FUN = sum)  rural <- hh_cereals[hh_cereals$Sector == "1", ] urban <- hh_cereals[hh_cereals$Sector == "2", ]  cat(sprintf(   "Weighted mean cereal expenditure (Rs/month):
  Rural: %.0f
  Urban: %.0f
",   weighted.mean(rural$Total_Consumption_Value, rural$Combined_Multiplier, na.rm = TRUE),   weighted.mean(urban$Total_Consumption_Value, urban$Combined_Multiplier, na.rm = TRUE) )) #> Weighted mean cereal expenditure (Rs/month): #>   Rural: 1486 #>   Urban: 1592

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: nesstar_export() writes one CSV (.csv.gz) per dataset directory. processes 50,000 rows per chunk default, even 4.65 GB T1 file require loading rows . Pass compress = TRUE (default) gzip-compressed output, typically halves file size negligible extra time.
code: output_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "nss68t2") nesstar_export(nb, output_dir = output_dir, compress = FALSE) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds47.csv (101651 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds48.csv (101651 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds49.csv (101651 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds50.csv (464730 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds51.csv (5277850 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds52.csv (1493313 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds53.csv (370071 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds54.csv (2343672 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds55.csv (3436786 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds56.csv (3317537 rows) #> Wrote: nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds57.csv (101647 rows) list.files(output_dir) #>  [1] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds47.csv" #>  [2] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds48.csv" #>  [3] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds49.csv" #>  [4] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds50.csv" #>  [5] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds51.csv" #>  [6] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds52.csv" #>  [7] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds53.csv" #>  [8] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds54.csv" #>  [9] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds55.csv" #> [10] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds56.csv" #> [11] "nss68_consumer_expenditure_type2_ds57.csv" nesstar_export(nb,                output_dir = output_dir,                datasets   = food_ds,                compress   = TRUE,                chunk_size = 100000L)

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: , took help colleague install Nesstar.exe asked convert binary stata file. code checks nesstarR’s CSV output matches .

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
code: stata_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "stata_68t2") unzip(stata_zip, exdir = stata_dir) dta_files <- sort(list.files(stata_dir, pattern = "\\.dta$",                               full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)) cat(length(dta_files), "Stata files found\n") #> 11 Stata files found

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: Stata archive labels file “Level N”. nesstarR names output files _dsNN. read column schemas Stata files (n_max = 0 loads header without row data) match level CSV normalised column name set, unique across 11 datasets. Expected output: every Stata level maps exactly one nesstarR CSV dataset.
code: library(haven)  # n_max = 0: read header only, no row data loaded stata_meta <- lapply(dta_files, function(f) {   df <- read_dta(f, n_max = 0L)   list(     path  = f,     level = as.integer(sub(".*[Ll]evel[_\\s-]*(\\d+).*", "\\1",                            basename(f), perl = TRUE)),     ncol  = ncol(df),     cols  = names(df)   ) })  csv_files <- sort(list.files(output_dir, pattern = "_ds\\d+\\.csv$",                               full.names = TRUE)) csv_meta <- lapply(csv_files, function(f) {   hdr    <- names(read.csv(f, nrows = 0L, check.names = FALSE))   ds_num <- as.integer(sub(".*_ds(\\d+)\\.csv$", "\\1", basename(f)))   list(path = f, ds_num = ds_num, cols = hdr) })  # STATA converts '.' in column names to '_'; normalise before comparing norm_names <- function(x) toupper(gsub("\\.", "_", trimws(x)))  for (sm in stata_meta) {   sn <- norm_names(sm$cols)   cm <- Filter(function(x) setequal(norm_names(x$cols), sn), csv_meta)   if (length(cm) == 0L) {     cat(sprintf("Level %2d  cols=%d  -> NO MATCH\n", sm$level, sm$ncol))     next   }   cat(sprintf("Level %2d -> ds%02d  cols=%d  cols_match=TRUE\n",               sm$level, cm[[1L]]$ds_num, sm$ncol)) } #> Level  5 -> ds51  cols=32  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  6 -> ds52  cols=29  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  4 -> ds50  cols=39  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  9 -> ds55  cols=36  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  7 -> ds53  cols=28  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  8 -> ds53  cols=28  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  3 -> ds49  cols=35  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  2 -> ds48  cols=42  cols_match=TRUE #> Level  1 -> ds47  cols=37  cols_match=TRUE #> Level 10 -> ds57  cols=41  cols_match=TRUE #> Level 11 -> ds56  cols=28  cols_match=TRUE

title: Reading NESSTAR survey data with nesstarR
text: Compare first 500 rows matched dataset cell cell. difference beyond floating-point epsilon (1e-9) flagged.
code: SPOT_ROWS <- 500L  for (sm in stata_meta) {   sn <- norm_names(sm$cols)   cm <- Filter(function(x) setequal(norm_names(x$cols), sn), csv_meta)   if (length(cm) == 0L) next   cm <- cm[[1L]]    s_df <- read_dta(sm$path, n_max = SPOT_ROWS)   c_df <- read.csv(cm$path, nrows = SPOT_ROWS, check.names = FALSE)    s_norm <- norm_names(names(s_df))   c_norm <- norm_names(names(c_df))   common <- intersect(s_norm, c_norm)    mismatches <- 0L   for (col in common) {     sv <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(             s_df[[which(s_norm == col)[1L]]])))     cv <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(             c_df[[which(c_norm == col)[1L]]])))     if (!all(is.na(sv)) && !all(is.na(cv))) {       mismatches <- mismatches +         sum(abs(sv - cv) > 1e-9, na.rm = TRUE) +         sum(is.na(sv) & !is.na(cv)) +         sum(!is.na(sv) & is.na(cv))     } else {       mismatches <- mismatches +         sum(trimws(as.character(sv)) != trimws(as.character(cv)), na.rm = TRUE)     }   }    cat(sprintf(     "Level %2d / ds%02d: %d cols x %d rows - %s\n",     sm$level, cm$ds_num, length(common), SPOT_ROWS,     if (mismatches == 0L) "all values match" else paste(mismatches, "MISMATCHES")   )) } #> Level  5 / ds51: 32 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level  6 / ds52: 29 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level  4 / ds50: 39 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level  9 / ds55: 36 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level  7 / ds53: 28 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level  8 / ds53: 28 cols x 500 rows - 6118 MISMATCHES #> Level  3 / ds49: 35 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level  2 / ds48: 42 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level  1 / ds47: 37 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level 10 / ds57: 41 cols x 500 rows - all values match #> Level 11 / ds56: 28 cols x 500 rows - all values match

title: nesstarR
text: Reads .Nesstar binary containers statistical agencies like MOSPI India R data frames exports CSV.

title: nesstarR
code: devtools::install_github("saketkc/nesstarR")

title: nesstarR
code: library(nesstarR)  x <- nesstar_parse("path/to/file.Nesstar") x #> NESSTAR container: file.Nesstar #> 3 dataset(s)  nesstar_datasets(x) #>   dataset_number row_count variable_count #> 1              1    500000             42 #> 2              2    120000             18 #> 3              3     80000             31  nesstar_variables(x, dataset_number = 1)  # Read a full dataset or just the columns you need df <- nesstar_read_dataset(x, dataset_number = 1) df_subset <- nesstar_read_dataset(x, dataset_number = 1, columns = c("AGE", "DISTRICT", "INCOME"))

title: nesstarR
text: NESSTAR files embed Huffman-compressed XML variable labels category codes: Older NSS-format files lack XML blocks; nesstar_metadata() returns NULL warning .
code: meta <- nesstar_metadata(x)  # one entry per dataset ds <- meta$datasets[[1]] ds$file_name       # original survey file name ds$variables[[1]]  # list(name, label, categories)

title: nesstarR
text: nesstar_export() writes dataset CSV file. reads 50,000 rows time default, large files don’t blow memory: function returns output file paths invisibly.
code: # All datasets, gzip-compressed (default) nesstar_export(x, output_dir = "./data") # Writes: data/file_ds1.csv.gz, data/file_ds2.csv.gz, ...  # Specific datasets, plain CSV, smaller chunks nesstar_export(   x,   output_dir = "./data",   datasets   = c(1, 2),   compress   = FALSE,   chunk_size = 10000L )

title: nesstarR
text: Two column types appear NESSTAR containers: Mode 1 (strings): fixed-width UTF-16LE fields, returned character Mode 5 (numerics): packed formats 4-bit nibbles IEEE 754 float64, returned numeric; missing values become NA parser auto-detects byte order (little-endian default, big-endian fallback) converts strings UTF-8.

NULL (default) every dataset exported. compress Logical; TRUE (default) output files gzip-compressed (.csv.gz). chunk_size Number rows process per iteration (reduces peak memory use).

text: named list: datasets list, one element per dataset, containing       dataset_number, file_name, variables (list       per-variable lists name, label,       categories). Returns NULL (warning) metadata decoded.

what: Read one dataset into an R data frame — nesstar_read_dataset
title: Read one dataset into an R data frame — nesstar_read_dataset
text: Numeric columns (mode 5) become numeric; string columns (mode 1) become character.  Missing values NA.

title: Read one dataset into an R data frame — nesstar_read_dataset
code: nesstar_read_dataset(x, dataset_number, columns = NULL)

title: Read one dataset into an R data frame — nesstar_read_dataset
text: x nesstar_binary object. dataset_number Integer dataset number. columns Optional character vector column names read.  NULL (default) columns returned.

title: Read one dataset into an R data frame — nesstar_read_dataset
text: data.frame.
